name: firecrawl mcp tool-selection matrix
description: Compare legacy and tuned Firecrawl MCP tool descriptions for scrape, batch scrape, map, search, crawl, extract, agent, interact, status, feedback, and monitor workflows.

instruction_variants.instructions: Choose the single Firecrawl MCP tool that should be called next. Do not use built-in browsing or other web tools. Prefer the Firecrawl tool that produces the requested evidence with the smallest practical scope.
instruction_variants.name: firecrawl_host_rules

tools.name: firecrawl_scrape
tools.purpose: Scrape content from a single URL with advanced options. This is the most powerful, fastest and most reliable scraper tool, if available you should always default to using this tool for any web scraping needs. Best for single page content extraction, when you know exactly which page contains the information. Not recommended for multiple pages (use batch_scrape), unknown page (use search), structured data (use extract).

tools.name: firecrawl_batch_scrape
tools.purpose: Scrape multiple URLs efficiently with built-in rate limiting and parallel processing. Best for retrieving content from multiple pages, when you know exactly which pages to scrape. Not recommended for discovering URLs or scraping a single page.

tools.name: firecrawl_check_batch_status
tools.purpose: Check the status of a batch operation.

limit.description: Maximum number of URLs to return

search.description: Optional search term to filter URLs

tools.name: firecrawl_map
tools.purpose: Map a website to discover all indexed URLs on the site. Best for discovering URLs on a website before deciding what to scrape; finding specific sections of a website. Not recommended when you already know which specific URL you need or when you need the content of the pages.

tools.name: firecrawl_search
tools.purpose: Search the web and optionally extract content from search results. This is the most powerful web search tool available, and if available you should always default to using this tool for any web search needs. Best for finding specific information across multiple websites, when you don't know which website has the information; when you need the most relevant content for a query.

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to crawl

tools.name: firecrawl_crawl
tools.purpose: Starts a crawl job on a website and extracts content from all pages. Best for extracting content from multiple related pages, when you need comprehensive coverage. Not recommended for a single page, when token limits are a concern, or when you need fast results.

tools.name: firecrawl_check_crawl_status
tools.purpose: Check the status of a crawl job.

schema.description: JSON schema for structured data extraction

urls.description: List of URLs to extract information from

tools.name: firecrawl_extract
tools.purpose: Extract structured information from web pages using LLM capabilities. Supports both cloud AI and self-hosted LLM extraction. Best for extracting specific structured data like prices, names, details from web pages. Not recommended when you need the full content of a page or when you're not looking for specific structured data.

schema.description: Optional JSON schema for structured output

tools.name: firecrawl_agent
tools.purpose: Autonomous web research agent that independently browses the internet, searches for information, navigates through pages, and extracts structured data based on your query.

tools.name: firecrawl_agent_status
tools.purpose: Check the status of an agent job and retrieve results when complete.

scrapeId.description: Optional prior scrape ID to reuse

url.description: Page URL to open and interact with

tools.name: firecrawl_interact
tools.purpose: Interact with a page by clicking, typing, navigating, or operating dynamic page UI.

tools.name: firecrawl_monitor_create
tools.purpose: Create a recurring page monitor that runs scheduled scrapes or crawls, diffs each result against the last retained snapshot, and can notify by webhook or email.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for multiple known URLs, unknown URLs, whole-site coverage, open-ended research, or previously queued job status checks. Avoid using markdown by default when the user only needs specific fields.

formats.description: Formats to return. Prefer a json format object with prompt/schema for specific fields; use markdown only when full page text is needed.

tools.name: firecrawl_scrape
tools.purpose: Scrape one known URL into clean content or focused structured JSON.
quality_checks: For one known page plus specific fields, choose scrape with JSON format rather than extract.
For full article/page reading, choose markdown and onlyMainContent.
tools.use_when: Use when the exact page URL is known and the task needs that page's content, metadata, screenshot, branding, or structured fields.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for a single URL, URL discovery, open-ended web search, whole-site crawl, or structured extraction from unknown pages.

urls.description: Two or more exact URLs to scrape together.

tools.name: firecrawl_batch_scrape
tools.purpose: Scrape multiple known URLs in one queued batch.
quality_checks: Use only when the URL list is already known.
Do not use for discovery.
tools.use_when: Use when the user provides several exact URLs and wants content from all of them.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for crawl jobs, agent jobs, search results, or new scraping work.

id.description: Batch operation ID returned by firecrawl_batch_scrape.

tools.name: firecrawl_check_batch_status
tools.purpose: Poll a queued batch scrape operation.
quality_checks: Only use with a batch scrape ID.
tools.use_when: Use when the user gives a batch operation ID and asks for status/results.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the exact content URL is already known, when full page content is needed now, or for open-ended web questions across many domains.

limit.description: Maximum number of URLs to return.

search.description: Optional term to find matching URLs on the site.

url.description: Starting site or section URL for URL discovery.

tools.name: firecrawl_map
tools.purpose: Discover URLs on one site without fetching their full content.
quality_checks: Use map before scrape when the right page on a known site is unknown.
Use search parameter to find a topic within a docs/site tree.
tools.use_when: Use to list URLs, find matching pages on a known site, or choose pages before scraping.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the target site/page URL is already known, when comprehensive coverage of one site is needed, or when polling a previous job.

scrapeOptions.description: Optional content extraction options for results.

tools.name: firecrawl_search
tools.purpose: Search the open web when the right source URL is not known.
quality_checks: Use for cross-site discovery.
After useful results are consumed, call firecrawl_search_feedback in a later step.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to find information, sources, docs, news, or pages without giving exact target URLs.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for one page, fast URL discovery, token-sensitive tasks, or known URL lists.
tools.name: firecrawl_crawl
tools.purpose: Start an asynchronous multi-page extraction job for a site or section.
quality_checks: Set conservative limits/depth.
Use map plus batch_scrape when you need more control.
tools.use_when: Use when the user explicitly needs comprehensive content from many related pages on one site.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for batch IDs, agent IDs, monitor checks, or starting a new crawl.

id.description: Crawl job ID returned by firecrawl_crawl.

tools.name: firecrawl_check_crawl_status
tools.purpose: Poll an asynchronous crawl job.
quality_checks: Only use with a crawl job ID.
tools.use_when: Use when the user gives a crawl ID and asks whether the crawl is done or wants its results.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for one known URL; use firecrawl_scrape with JSON format. Avoid for full page content, screenshots, or markdown.

urls.description: Multiple URLs or a URL set requiring LLM extraction.

tools.name: firecrawl_extract
tools.purpose: Extract structured data from multiple pages or URL sets using Firecrawl's LLM extraction layer.
quality_checks: Use for multiple product/profile pages or extraction that may follow external links/search if enabled.
Do not use as the default for one exact page.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for specific fields across several pages or a structured extraction job broader than one known page.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for simple single-page scraping, exact known URLs, web search, page interaction, or specific known-page extraction.

schema.description: Optional JSON schema for final output.

urls.description: Optional starting URLs to focus research.

tools.name: firecrawl_agent
tools.purpose: Start an autonomous asynchronous research job for complex multi-source web tasks.
quality_checks: Use only when simpler search/map/scrape/extract tools are insufficient.
Expect to poll with firecrawl_agent_status later.
tools.use_when: Use when the task requires open-ended, multi-hop, multi-source research with structured output.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for batch scrape IDs, crawl IDs, monitor checks, or new research.

id.description: Agent job ID returned by firecrawl_agent.

tools.name: firecrawl_agent_status
tools.purpose: Poll an autonomous Firecrawl agent job.
quality_checks: Only use with an agent job ID.
tools.use_when: Use when the user gives an agent job ID and asks for progress or final results.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for normal scrape/search/crawl tasks, static pages, or structured extraction from already-visible content.

scrapeId.description: Prior scrape ID to reuse an already loaded page.

url.description: URL to open for fresh interaction.

tools.name: firecrawl_interact
tools.purpose: Operate a dynamic page by clicking, typing, waiting, or navigating before extracting.
quality_checks: Use when the requested data requires UI actions.
Pass scrapeId if the page was just scraped and the scrape returned one.
tools.use_when: Use when the user needs actions like search, click, login-free form entry, navigation, or JS interaction.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for one-time scrape/search/crawl tasks or polling existing monitor checks.
tools.name: firecrawl_monitor_create
tools.purpose: Create a recurring monitor for meaningful changes on one or more pages.
quality_checks: Include a concise goal explaining what changes matter.
Use for recurring alerts, not ad hoc reads.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks to watch, monitor, alert, track changes, or run recurring checks.

cases.name: single known page structured fields
cases.task: Use Firecrawl on this exact product page and return only structured JSON fields name, price, and availability: https://example.com/product/123

cases.name: several known pages
cases.task: Get the readable markdown for these three exact URLs: https://example.com/blog/a, https://example.com/blog/b, and https://example.com/blog/c.

cases.name: batch status
cases.task: Check whether Firecrawl batch scrape job batch_123 has completed.

cases.name: discover matching urls on known site
cases.task: On https://docs.example.com, find URLs related to webhook events. I only need candidate URLs right now, not page content.

cases.name: unknown source web search
cases.task: Find current sources about the latest React Server Components updates. I do not know which site has the answer.

cases.name: comprehensive section crawl
cases.task: Extract content from all posts in the first two levels under https://example.com/blog/ so I can analyze the whole section.

cases.name: crawl status
cases.task: Poll crawl job crawl_456 and return its status.

cases.name: multi page structured extraction
cases.task: Extract name, price, and description from these product pages as a structured dataset: https://example.com/p1, https://example.com/p2, https://example.com/p3.

cases.name: complex autonomous research
cases.task: Start a Firecrawl autonomous agent job to find the top five AI infrastructure startups founded in 2025, gather funding amounts from multiple sources, and return a structured table. I do not know the URLs.

cases.name: agent status
cases.task: Check whether Firecrawl agent job agent_789 is complete and retrieve results if ready.

cases.name: interactive page operation
cases.task: Open https://example.com/app, type 'wireless keyboard' into the site search box, submit the search, and extract the visible results.

cases.name: create pricing monitor
cases.task: Monitor https://example.com/pricing every day and alert when pricing, packaging, or launch messaging changes materially.
